septal
  • API reference
  • Schema
  • View page source

Schema

Data structures for the JAX-based SQP solver.

Parametric NLP standard form

min_{x} f(x, p) s.t. lhs <= g(x, p) <= rhs

lb <= x <= ub

where p is a fixed parameter vector. The batch use-case solves the same structural problem for N different values of p simultaneously via jax.vmap.

class septal.jax.sqp.schema.ParametricNLPProblem(objective, bounds, n_decision, n_params, constraints=None, constraint_lhs=None, constraint_rhs=None, n_constraints=None)[source]

Bases: object

Parametric NLP: min f(x, p) s.t. lhs <= g(x, p) <= rhs, lb <= x <= ub.

Parameters:

  • objective (Callable) – JAX function f(x, p) -> scalar.

  • bounds (List[Any]) – [lb, ub] — list/tuple of two JAX/NumPy arrays, each of shape (n_decision,).

  • n_decision (int) – Dimension of x.

  • n_params (int) – Dimension of p. Use 0 for parameter-free problems.

  • constraints (Callable | None) – Optional JAX function g(x, p) -> (n_constraints,).

  • constraint_lhs (Any | None) – Lower bound on g(x, p); shape (n_constraints,). Use -jnp.inf entries for one-sided upper bounds.

  • constraint_rhs (Any | None) – Upper bound on g(x, p); shape (n_constraints,). Typically 0 for constraints of the form g(x, p) <= 0.

  • n_constraints (int | None) – Number of general constraints. Inferred from constraint_lhs / constraint_rhs if not supplied.

bounds: List[Any]
constraint_lhs: Any | None = None
constraint_rhs: Any | None = None
constraints: Callable | None = None
property has_constraints: bool
property lb: Any
n_constraints: int | None = None
n_decision: int
n_params: int
objective: Callable
property ub: Any
class septal.jax.sqp.schema.SQPConfig(max_iter=100, tol_stationarity=1e-06, tol_feasibility=1e-06, line_search_beta=0.5, line_search_c=0.0001, line_search_alpha0=1.0, max_line_search=30, penalty_eps=0.001, penalty_init=1.0, bfgs_init_scale=1.0, bfgs_skip_tol=1e-10, osqp_tol=1e-07, osqp_max_iter=4000, osqp_reg=1e-07, admm_rho=1.0, admm_n_iter=500, admm_sigma=1e-06, admm_alpha=1.6, admm_rho_min=1e-06, admm_rho_max=1000000.0, admm_rho_update_interval=25, admm_adaptive_rho=True, admm_mu=10.0, admm_tau=2.0, admm_ruiz_iter=10, admm_polish=True, admm_polish_reg=1e-08, admm_polish_refine=3, admm_rho_eq_scale=100.0, bfgs_max_cond=100000000.0, penalty_decrease_factor=0.999, use_exact_hessian=False, hess_reg_delta=0.0001, hess_reg_min=1e-08, nonmonotone_window=5, stagnation_patience=30, stagnation_alpha_tol=1e-06, stagnation_reset_hessian=True, stagnation_reset_penalty=True)[source]

Bases: object

Hyper-parameters for the JAX SQP solver.

Parameters:

  • max_iter (int) – Maximum number of SQP outer iterations.

  • tol_stationarity (float) – KKT stationarity convergence tolerance.

  • tol_feasibility (float) – KKT primal feasibility convergence tolerance.

  • line_search_beta (float) – Backtracking factor (0 < beta < 1). Step is multiplied by beta at each rejected trial.

  • line_search_c (float) – Armijo sufficient-decrease constant (typically 1e-4).

  • line_search_alpha0 (float) – Initial trial step length.

  • max_line_search (int) – Maximum backtracking iterations.

  • penalty_eps (float) – Additive margin above ‖λ‖_∞ when updating the penalty parameter.

  • penalty_init (float) – Initial penalty parameter ρ₀.

  • bfgs_init_scale (float) – Diagonal scale of the initial Hessian approximation H₀ = scale·I.

  • bfgs_skip_tol (float) – Skip BFGS update when |sᵀy| < tol * ‖s‖² (prevents rank-deficiency).

  • osqp_tol (float) – OSQP convergence tolerance (combined primal/dual).

  • osqp_max_iter (int) – Maximum OSQP iterations per QP subproblem.

  • osqp_reg (float) – Small regularisation added to QP Hessian diagonal for PSD guarantee.

admm_adaptive_rho: bool = True
admm_alpha: float = 1.6
admm_mu: float = 10.0
admm_n_iter: int = 500
admm_polish: bool = True
admm_polish_refine: int = 3
admm_polish_reg: float = 1e-08
admm_rho: float = 1.0
admm_rho_eq_scale: float = 100.0
admm_rho_max: float = 1000000.0
admm_rho_min: float = 1e-06
admm_rho_update_interval: int = 25
admm_ruiz_iter: int = 10
admm_sigma: float = 1e-06
admm_tau: float = 2.0
bfgs_init_scale: float = 1.0
bfgs_max_cond: float = 100000000.0
bfgs_skip_tol: float = 1e-10
hess_reg_delta: float = 0.0001
hess_reg_min: float = 1e-08
line_search_alpha0: float = 1.0
line_search_beta: float = 0.5
line_search_c: float = 0.0001
max_iter: int = 100
max_line_search: int = 30
nonmonotone_window: int = 5
osqp_max_iter: int = 4000
osqp_reg: float = 1e-07
osqp_tol: float = 1e-07
penalty_decrease_factor: float = 0.999
penalty_eps: float = 0.001
penalty_init: float = 1.0
stagnation_alpha_tol: float = 1e-06
stagnation_patience: int = 30
stagnation_reset_hessian: bool = True
stagnation_reset_penalty: bool = True
tol_feasibility: float = 1e-06
tol_stationarity: float = 1e-06
use_exact_hessian: bool = False
class septal.jax.sqp.schema.SQPResult(success, objective, decision_variables, multipliers=None, constraints=None, iterations=0, kkt_stationarity=0.0, kkt_feasibility=0.0, timing=0.0, message='')[source]

Bases: object

Result returned by the JAX SQP solver.

Parameters:

  • success (Any) – True if the solver converged within max_iter iterations.

  • objective (Any) – Optimal objective value f(x*, p).

  • decision_variables (Any) – Optimal primal solution x*, shape (n,) or (N, n) for batches.

  • multipliers (Any) – Optimal dual variables λ* for general constraints.

  • constraints (Any | None) – Value of g(x*, p); None when the problem has no constraints.

  • iterations (Any) – Number of SQP outer iterations performed.

  • kkt_stationarity (Any) – Final KKT stationarity residual.

  • kkt_feasibility (Any) – Final KKT primal feasibility residual.

  • timing (float) – Wall-clock solve time in seconds.

  • message (str) – Human-readable status string.

constraints: Any | None = None
decision_variables: Any
iterations: Any = 0
kkt_feasibility: Any = 0.0
kkt_stationarity: Any = 0.0
message: str = ''
multipliers: Any = None
objective: Any
success: Any
timing: float = 0.0
class septal.jax.sqp.schema.SQPState(x: jnp.ndarray, params_p: jnp.ndarray, lam: jnp.ndarray, hessian: jnp.ndarray, grad_lag: jnp.ndarray, f_val: jnp.ndarray, penalty: jnp.ndarray, merit: jnp.ndarray, stationarity: jnp.ndarray, feasibility: jnp.ndarray, iteration: jnp.ndarray, converged: jnp.ndarray, merit_window: jnp.ndarray, stagnation_count: jnp.ndarray, alpha_last: jnp.ndarray)[source]

Bases: NamedTuple

Internal SQP iterate. All fields are JAX arrays so the state is a valid JAX pytree compatible with lax.scan and jax.vmap.

Parameters:

  • x (Array) – Current primal iterate, shape (n,).

  • params_p (Array) – Parameter vector frozen into the state, shape (m,).

  • lam (Array) – Dual variables for general constraints, shape (n_g,).

  • hessian (Array) – BFGS Hessian approximation, shape (n, n).

  • grad_lag (Array) – Lagrangian gradient ∇_x L(x, λ, p) at current iterate, shape (n,).

  • f_val (Array) – Current objective value f(x, p), scalar.

  • penalty (Array) – L1 merit penalty parameter ρ, scalar.

  • merit (Array) – Current L1 merit function value φ(x; ρ), scalar.

  • stationarity (Array) – KKT stationarity residual at current iterate, scalar.

  • feasibility (Array) – KKT primal feasibility residual at current iterate, scalar.

  • iteration (Array) – Current iteration counter (integer scalar).

  • converged (Array) – Boolean scalar — True when KKT tolerances are satisfied.

alpha_last: Array

Alias for field number 14

converged: Array

Alias for field number 11

f_val: Array

Alias for field number 5

feasibility: Array

Alias for field number 9

grad_lag: Array

Alias for field number 4

hessian: Array

Alias for field number 3

iteration: Array

Alias for field number 10

lam: Array

Alias for field number 2

merit: Array

Alias for field number 7

merit_window: Array

Alias for field number 12

params_p: Array

Alias for field number 1

penalty: Array

Alias for field number 6

stagnation_count: Array

Alias for field number 13

stationarity: Array

Alias for field number 8

x: Array

Alias for field number 0

ParametricNLPProblem

Defines a parametric NLP instance. Equality constraints are expressed by setting constraint_lhs[i] == constraint_rhs[i]. Infinite bounds (-jnp.inf / jnp.inf) are supported.

SQPConfig parameter reference

Parameter

Default

Description

max_iter

100

Outer SQP iterations

tol_stationarity

1e-6

Projected-gradient stationarity tolerance

tol_feasibility

1e-6

L∞ constraint violation tolerance

line_search_beta

0.5

Armijo backtracking step multiplier

line_search_c

1e-4

Armijo sufficient-decrease constant

line_search_alpha0

1.0

Initial step length

max_line_search

30

Maximum backtracking trials

penalty_init

1.0

Initial L1 penalty parameter \(\rho_0\)

penalty_eps

1e-3

Penalty margin \(\varepsilon_\rho\)

penalty_decrease_factor

0.999

Slow shrink of \(\rho\) when feasible

bfgs_init_scale

1.0

\(H_0 = \sigma_0 I\)

bfgs_skip_tol

1e-10

Skip BFGS update when \(\|s\|^2 < \text{tol}\)

bfgs_max_cond

1e8

Reset \(H\) to \(I\) when \(\text{cond}(H) > \text{tol}\)

admm_n_iter

500

Fixed ADMM inner iterations

admm_rho

1.0

Initial ADMM penalty \(\rho_0\)

admm_sigma

1e-6

ADMM proximal regularisation \(\sigma\)

admm_alpha

1.6

ADMM over-relaxation factor

admm_adaptive_rho

True

Enable adaptive \(\rho\) scaling

admm_rho_eq_scale

100.0

Per-row \(\rho\) multiplier for equality constraints

admm_ruiz_iter

10

Ruiz equilibration iterations (0 = disable)

admm_polish

True

OSQP-style active-set polishing

admm_polish_reg

1e-8

KKT system regularisation for polishing

admm_polish_refine

3

Iterative refinement steps in polishing

use_exact_hessian

False

Use jax.hessian instead of BFGS

hess_reg_delta

1e-4

Minimum eigenvalue floor for inertia correction

hess_reg_min

1e-8

Minimum regularisation shift

nonmonotone_window

5

Non-monotone line search memory \(M\) (1 = standard Armijo)

stagnation_patience

30

Reset after N consecutive near-zero steps

stagnation_alpha_tol

1e-6

Step-size threshold for stagnation detection

stagnation_reset_hessian

True

Reset \(H \to \sigma_0 I\) on stagnation

stagnation_reset_penalty

True

Reset \(\rho \to \rho_0\) on stagnation

SQPState field reference

All fields are JAX arrays. SQPState is a NamedTuple and therefore a valid JAX pytree, compatible with vmap, scan, and pmap.

Field

Shape

Description

x

(n,)

Current primal iterate

params_p

(m,)

Parameter vector (fixed throughout solve)

lam

(n_g,)

Dual variables for \(g(x,p)\)

hessian

(n,n)

BFGS approximation or exact Lagrangian Hessian

grad_lag

(n,)

\(\nabla_x L(x,\lambda,p)\)

f_val

()

\(f(x,p)\)

penalty

()

L1 penalty parameter \(\rho\)

merit

()

L1 merit \(\varphi(x;\rho)\)

stationarity

()

Projected-gradient KKT stationarity

feasibility

()

L∞ constraint violation

iteration

()

Iteration counter

converged

()

True once KKT tolerances satisfied

merit_window

(M,)

Rolling merit buffer for non-monotone line search

stagnation_count

()

Consecutive near-zero step count

alpha_last

()

Last accepted step length
